stem: A telecommunications communication network uses 3 parallel relays for redundancy. The message delivered requires at least one relay to function. relay 1 has a reliability between 67% and 75% (uncertain due to limited operational data). relay 2 has a reliability of 87%. relay 3 has a reliability of 79%. What is the overall probability that the message delivered?

ground_truth.answer: P(message delivered) ranges from 0.991 to 0.9932. Point estimate: 0.9921.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 relays fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.33 × 0.13 × 0.21 = 0.009. P(works) = 0.991.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.25 × 0.13 × 0.21 = 0.0068. P(works) = 0.9932.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
